narrative.title: Why medical sales, why Smart Care
narrative.body:
I spent the first part of my career in institutional finance, most recently at Goldman Sachs, where I managed multi million dollar client relationships. I enjoyed many parts of my financial roles, but I always sensed there was a career out there that fit my personality even better. In 2023 I left to become a full time parent.

In 2025 I watched a stranger save someone's life during a medical emergency, and that moment stayed with me. It pushed me to get my EMT license. I spent countless hours in hospitals, including one rotation assisting a physician during emergency surgery, and I kept seeing the same problem everywhere: nurses stretched too thin, physicians pulled in a dozen directions at once, a constant stream of alarms and notifications, and patients who needed monitoring without enough staff to provide it. I came to care deeply about the medical world, and I wanted to be part of the solution.

I've always been achievement oriented and tenacious about whatever I take on, so when a friend told me my personality was built for medical sales, it clicked. The first thing I did was look for a product I could genuinely believe in. That search led me to Smart Care, where I learned about the Vocera software platforms, apps and badges, care.ai Ambient Sensors, Virtual Nursing and Monitoring, and the Virtual Command Center. I've seen firsthand what even one of these products can mean for a hospital, and I want to help bring Smart Care to as many places as possible, improving outcomes for patients, clinicians, hospitals, and beyond.

That mission is why I focused on North Texas, where coverage hasn't kept up with need. Don covers twelve to fifteen counties alone, and the entire South region has only eight to nine reps across multiple states. Texas Health Resources recently closed a multi million dollar deal with Care.ai integrated into their beds. Children's Hospital is also a client, and Southwestern already runs ProCuity beds, badges, Engage, and Care.ai, and is now opening a mental health hospital and wanting more badges. Baylor Scott and White came to Don asking about the products before he ever reached out. He's also closed a smaller orthopedic clinic deal, which shows the platform moves downmarket too. That's a good problem to have, but it means Don's time goes to the people already calling, while the accounts that haven't reached out yet, smaller clinics and the verticals beyond hospitals this platform can serve, like aged care, veterinary, hospitality, retail, power plants, and libraries, go unworked. I'm not asking to split Don's territory. I'm asking to find and build the accounts he doesn't have time to chase, the same way Don told me he built his own: network in and ask each contact for the next name. Stryker already has the trust. What this territory needs is someone finding the accounts before competitors do.

recommendations.label: Stryker

recommendations.text: She brings the kind of drive, curiosity, and relationship-building mindset that translates to medical sales success.

recommendations.title: Product Manager, NPD, Stryker
recommendations.text: Kelsey has a unique drive, customer-first mindset and attention to detail that differentiates her from other candidates. She would perform with utmost accountability, integrity, and customer-centric mindset.

recommendations.label: EMT Instructor
recommendations.name: Aditya Kanakasabapathy
recommendations.title: EMT Instructor, University of Texas at Dallas
recommendations.text: She is capable of having a conversation about medical products with the relevant professionals on a really intelligent level, viewed through the patient care and clinical realities lens, which will facilitate her making a true connection with healthcare professionals.

recommendations.label: iRhythm
recommendations.name: Justin Atchison
recommendations.title: Territory Manager, iRhythm Technologies
recommendations.text: I was immediately impressed by her communication skills, her genuine curiosity about the industry, and her potential as a sales professional. She asked thoughtful questions about the clinical side and showed real interest in how it impacts physicians and patients.

startStories.title: Stakeholder to C-Suite Relationships
startStories.teaser: Build and maintain executive and key customer relationships through the C-suite to build trust and close deals.
startStories.body: At Goldman Sachs, I managed syndicated loan contracts and presented directly to institutional clients and internal stakeholders, where a single misread detail could cost the trust of the relationship. That built real executive presence, the ability to confidently talk with senior decision makers, anticipate their concerns, and hold their trust through a complex process. I bring that same discipline to building relationships from scrub techs to C-suite that close deals.

startStories.title: Territory Planning
startStories.teaser: Plan and implement effective territory sales strategy required to progress deals through the process.
startStories.body:
During an 18 month volunteer representative role across Nebraska, Iowa, and South Dakota, our quota was defined by how many people we could convert into members of our organization. I inherited an underperforming territory and turned it into one of the best through territory research, stakeholder mapping, active listening for the need, and an adaptive approach when the obvious path didn't work, a skillset that comes directly from my Gallup Strategic strength.

That same instinct carried into how I approached Smart Care. Studying the platform's wearables and workflow tools, I found a product I genuinely believe in, and I'd bring that same strategic approach to the team, learning from coaching and feedback along the way to become the best rep for my area.

startStories.title: Sales Cycle Urgency
startStories.teaser: Advance opportunities efficiently and with a sense of urgency through the sales cycle.
startStories.body: As an EMT finishing in the top 5% of my class, I trained in emergency rooms alongside physicians. During one rotation, a doctor gave rapid instructions during an emergency surgery situation. With the nurses occupied, he told the three EMT students watching from the corner of the room to jump in and help. In a moment with zero margin for error, I was the only one who moved. That's my Activator strength in practice, acting as soon as the need is clear. I bring that same instinct to a sales cycle: strategic preparation, then action.

skills.label: Clinical
skills.items: Physician engagement · Hospital systems navigation · High acuity clinical fluency · Patient-facing experience · EMT certified

skills.label: Execution
skills.items: Territory ownership · Complex sales cycle management · CRM pipeline management · Forecasting and quota attainment · Data-driven decisions

skills.label: Stakeholder
skills.items: Executive and C suite stakeholder management · Consultative selling · Cross-functional collaboration · Negotiation and deal closing · Trust building

skills.label: Languages & Credentials
skills.items: Spanish — professional working proficiency, 18-month immersive deployment · Licensed EMT · BLS Certified · FEMA Certified

experience.title: Emergency Medical Technician — Clinical Rotations
experience.body: Top 5% of class. Working alongside physicians in the ER, trauma bay, and in-transit care taught me to read a room fast, communicate clearly under pressure, and earn trust with people who have no time for hesitation. Assisting during a live emergency tracheotomy and stabilizing patients during a helipad to trauma bay handoff sharpened the same instinct that closes deals: stay calm, act decisively, and never lose the thread of what the person in front of you actually needs.

experience.title: Global Markets Analyst
experience.body: Managing 20+ institutional relationships across multi-million dollar syndicated loan portfolios, in a 70+ hour week with zero room for error, built the executive presence and precision that complex, multi stakeholder sales cycles demand. Independently catching a contract misinterpretation others missed, resolving it, and aligning internal leadership around the fix taught me how to hold a senior relationship together under pressure, not just maintain it.

experience.title: Business Operations Analyst
experience.body: Diagnosing territory level performance gaps and building executive forecasting models in SQL, Snowflake, and Python taught me how to turn raw data into a business case a C suite will actually act on. Sizing and selling a $2M+ opportunity to senior leadership takes the same skill set territory ownership and quota forecasting require: know the numbers cold, then make the case.

experience.title: Executive Director (Volunteer) — BYU Chapter
experience.body: Leading a 5 person team overseeing 100+ volunteer advocates across a high stakes judicial environment built the cross functional coordination and stakeholder management skills required to keep a complex, multi party process moving toward the right outcome for everyone involved.

experience.title: Spanish-Speaking Representative — Nebraska, Iowa & South Dakota
experience.body: Inheriting an underperforming 3 state territory and building it into one of the strongest through stakeholder mapping, active listening, and a CRM pipeline I built myself is the same territory strategy playbook I'd bring to a designated sales territory: research the ground, find the real need, and adapt when the obvious approach doesn't work.
